english: ma* (ma) W. the following day; *tiṅ-jug* (ting jug) remaining part, the last remainder, *di-riṅ ja tiṅ-jug len soṅ* (di ring ja ting jug len song) W. to-day I have used the last of my tea. -- rtiṅ-pa (rting pa) 1. the end, extremity, lowest part, e.g. of a stick Glr.; gen.: 2. the heel of the foot, rtiṅ-lčags (rting lcags) a spur, rtiṅ-lčags rgyáb-pa (rting lcags rgyab pa) to prick with the spurs, to spur; rtiṅ-ču (rting chu) the Achilles-tendon.
pf. brtibs (brtibs), fut. brtib (brtib), imp. *tib(s)* (tib(s)) to break or pull down (cf. rdib-pa (rdib pa)).

1. excrement, dirt rtug-skám (rtug skam) or -ském (- skem) dry excrements Med.; rgyal-srid rtug-pa bżin-du dór-ba (rgyal srid rtug pa bzhin du dor ba) to throw off royalty like dirt Pth.; rtug-pa pyís-pai rdo (rtug pa phyis pa'i rdo) a stone for wiping one's self Mil. -- 2. C. wind, flatulence. -- 3. (b)rtug ((b)rtug) v. sub tógs-pa (rtogs pa).
pf. brtuṅs (brtungs), fut. brtuṅ (brtung), also stuṅ-ba (stung ba), to make shorter, to shorten, to contract, e.g. a rope, a dress; rnyá-ba brtuṅs (rnya ba brtungs) his neck is contracted Mng.
v. ytun (gtun); rtun-ril (rtun ril) a trituration-bowl Sch.
, brtún-pa (brtun pa), diligence, rtún-pa skyed-pa (rtun pa skyed pa) to be diligent Zam. Cf. dún-pa (dun pa).

or rtúl-ba (rtul ba), blunt, dull, mtson-rtúl (mtshon rtul) a blunt weapon Cs.; gen. fig.: dbáṅ-po rtúl-po (dbang po rtul po) (opp. to rnón-po (rnon po) or rnó-ba (rno ba) sharp, and °bríṅ-po ('bring po) middling) dullness, stupidity, imbecility of mind; dull, stupid; blo-rtúl (blo rtul) weak intellect. -- (b)rtul-pód-pa ((b)rtul pod pa) boldness, courage; bold, brave Dzl.
foal, colt, rteu braṅ-ba (rte'u brang ba) to bring forth a colt, to foal Cs.

(cf. the next article) that which contains, keeps, or supports a thing, 1. a hold, support, esp. in compounds: ka-rtén (ka rten) the plinth or base of a pillar Cs.; rkaṅ-rtén (rkang rten) (resp. żabs-rten (zhabs rten)) a foot-stool Cs.; żu-rtén (zhu rten) a present given to support a supplication, and never omitted by Orientals when making a petition; *sem-tén* (*sem ten*) W. token, keep-sake; -- esp. a visible representation, a statue or figure of Buddha or of other divine beings, which the pious may take hold of, and to which their devotions are more immediately directed (v. the explanation in Glr. chp. II, init.) -- 2. receptacle, resp. yduṅ-rtén (gdung rten), for the bones or relics of a saint, mčód-rten (mchod rten) for oblations, v. mčód-pa (mchod pa), compounds; rig-pai rten (rig pa'i rten) receptacle of the soul, i.e. the body Schr.; rig-pa rtén-med-pa (rig pa rten med pa), rten daṅ brál-ba (rten dang bral ba) the houseless, bodiless soul Thgr.; °jig-rtén ('jig rten) v. °jig ('jig); snyiṅ ni tse srog séms-kyi rten (snying ni tshe srog sems kyi rten) the heart is the seat of life and of the soul Mng.; seat, abode, residence, of a deity, sanctuary, temple (Dzl.), shrine, rtén-gyi ytsó-bo (rten gyi gtso bo) the deity residing in a shrine Glr.; visible representation, symbol, of divine objects or beings, esp. the rten ysum (rten gsum): skú-rten (sku rten) an image of Buddha, ysúṅ-rten (gsung rten) symbol of the doctrine, gen. consisting in a volume of the holy writings, túgs-rten (thugs rten) symbol of grace, a pyramid, Köpp. II, 294. Hence rten might very suitably be used for denoting the material element in the Christian sacraments, viz. the water, and the bread and wine. -- 3. present, gift, prop. for żú-rten (zhu rten) (v. sub no. 1), and then in a more general sense, resp. pyag-rtén (phyag rten), W., for pyag-rtágs (phyag rtags); also offering, oblation. -- 4. sex, specified as male, female, or hermaphrodite; independently of age S.g.; sometimes comprising age S.g.; or denoting age alone, as child, man, old man Lt.; calling, situation in life Tar. 163, 15 (where gyi (gyi) ought to be changed into ni (ni)) 176, 15; 178, 18; some compounds follow still at the end of the next article.

1. vb., pf. and fut. brten (brten), imp. rton (rton) (brten? (brten?)), to keep, to hold, to adhere to, to lean on, kár-ba-la (mkhar ba la) on a staff Pth.; ká-ba-la (ka ba la) against a pillar; lág-pa grám-pa-la (lag pa 'gram pa la) to lean one's head on one's hand, in meditating Dzl.; fig. to depend or rely on, brtén-pai blá-ma (brten pa'i bla ma) the priest to whom one holds; snúm-la rtén-pa (snum la rten pa) to keep to the fat, i.e. to eat much fat Med.; kríg-pai čós-la (khrig pa'i chos la) to be given, addicted, to sensuality; *ču táṅ-wa máṅ-po-la tén-na* (chu thang wa mang po la ten na) C. if
